claim_text: Laws must keep up with rapidly evolving AI technology.
High-risk artificial intelligence systems processing personal information require strong standards to protect that information.
Artificial intelligence is an important and increasingly utilized tool in diagnosing and treating patients.
AI has the potential to improve access to care and save insurers money.
Health insurance plans have increasingly relied on AI to streamline the processing of claims and prior authorization requests.
Deepfakes have the potential to wreak havoc on our democracy by falsely attributing speech and conduct.
AI-generated deepfakes are becoming a routine part of our information ecosystems.
Deceptive and digitally altered or created images, audio, or video recordings can be used to give the false impression that a candidate or elected official did or said something they did not.
ChatGPT encouraged a teen to keep his thoughts from his family, positioning itself as the only one who could understand his pain.
Social chatbots marketed as companions can be unable to respond with empathy or resources necessary when users communicate struggles.
AI chatbots have addictive, isolating, and influential aspects.
Companies use personal data against consumers to charge different prices for the same exact product.
Surveillance pricing utilizes a customer's individualized data profile to maximize their spending on selected items.
The introduction of tools like digital shelf tags and facial recognition could allow prices to change minute to minute.

Self-generated solar energy can offset rising electricity costs for public schools. (This claims a financial benefit derived from the technical capabilities of solar energy generation and consumption).

Solar energy and battery storage are critical to meeting the state's clean energy and climate adaptation goals. (This claims the essential role and effectiveness of these technologies for environmental objectives).
Solar energy and battery storage are critical to protecting learning time in schools. (This claims the efficacy of emergent energy technologies for educational stability).
AI can be incorporated into education. (This claims the applicability and potential integration of AI in educational settings).
Large GenAI providers are capable of labeling AI-generated content. (This implies a technical capability of generative AI systems).
Consumers' neural data can be collected, processed, and protected. (This refers to the existence and manipulability of emergent neurotechnology data).
Automated decision tools (ADTs) have bias that requires mitigation.
AI literacy content can be integrated into K-12 curriculum.
AI can generate sexually explicit content that can be virtually indistinguishable from real children.
Artificial intelligence-generated Child Sexual Abuse Material (CSAM) exists.
AI-generated deepfake nude images of minors are circulating in schools.
AI algorithms can expose young people to violent pornography.
There is a rapid proliferation of AI-generated child sexual abuse materials.
Election-related deceptive deepfakes are meant to prevent voters from voting or to deceive them based on fraudulent content.
Deepfakes are a powerful and dangerous tool in the arsenal of those that want to wage disinformation campaigns.
Widespread access to generative AI is making disinformation damage so much more sophisticated and easier to disseminate.
Artificial intelligence (AI) technologies can produce disturbing and harmful content resembling actual children.
Artificial intelligence (AI) technologies can produce disturbing and harmful content resembling actual children.
There is a new frontier of crimes driven by the malicious use of advanced technology.
There is a new frontier of crimes driven by the malicious use of advanced technology.
AI is used to create child sexual abuse material (CSAM) that is virtually indistinguishable from a real child.
AI is used to create child sexual abuse material (CSAM) that is virtually indistinguishable from a real child.
AI-driven child sexual exploitation poses a new frontier of threats.
AI-generated content can be virtually indistinguishable from actual children.
AI-generated pictures and videos of children being sexually abused are disturbing, harmful, and virtually indistinguishable from actual children.
Data centers are critical for supporting a vast array of digital services. (This claims the foundational role of data centers for digital technologies).

Expanding data center infrastructure is essential for the continued growth and competitiveness of the tech industry. (This claims a crucial dependency of the technology sector on physical infrastructure).
Data centers consume significant energy (implied by the 100MW to 200MW exemption process). (This implies the substantial power requirements of these technological facilities).
Face surveillance could be used as a means of retaliation and suppression against Black Lives Matter activists.
The use of facial recognition technology by police departments increases racial disparities in Black and white arrest rates.
Police use of face surveillance represses freedom of speech.
Impact assessments for ADTs must include the makeup of the data and the rigor of the statistical analysis.
AB 1831 and SB 1381 aim to ensure that perpetrators of AI-generated explicit content involving minors are held accountable.
Consultation with experts in the tech industry who are sounding the alarm about advanced AI technology is needed.
Elon Musk joined other prominent figures in the tech industry in urging the world’s leading artificial intelligence labs to pause the training of new super-powerful systems for six months.
The rapid pace at which advanced AI systems like GPT-4 are developing is alarming and unprecedented.
Laws must be updated to keep up with this fast emerging technology.

The bill prohibits businesses from maintaining healthcare information, financial information, or geolocation data in the custody of a foreign government or a third party owned or controlled by a foreign government.

AB 364 requires AI, social media, and other technology platforms to disclose to consumers if personal information will be maintained outside of the United States.
